title: Surah Ar-Rahman Decoded: The Question God Asks Two Creations

sound.ambient: calm paradise garden ambience, soft flowing water, gentle airy breeze, distant soft birdsong, serene and clean, no music, no melody, no instruments

_note: Surah Decoded #4 - Ar-Rahman (REDESIGNED for genuine insight + accuracy). Bespoke sourced graphics: rahman-rhythm (refrain x31), rahman-dual (the refrain is DUAL -> mankind + jinn, proven by 55:33), rahman-gardens (2+2 gardens, 55:46 + 55:62). Genuine poll (the dual reveal, NOT pre-answered). Verses 55:1-4, 55:13, 55:33, 55:26-27 auto-pulled. Decoded -> app ad. Quran-only. No em dashes.

segments.text: There is a question Allah asks thirty-one times in a single chapter. And most people miss who He is actually asking. Let us decode Surah Ar-Rahman.
segments.caption: One question, 31 times.

segments.text: Surah Ar-Rahman. The Most Merciful. The fifty-fifth chapter of the Quran.
It opens with mercy itself. The Most Merciful. He taught the Quran. He created man. And He taught him to speak.
Then it moves like a heartbeat. A gift, then a question. Another gift, and the same question again. Thirty-one times in all.
So which of the favors of your Lord will you deny?

data.question: This question is in the DUAL form. Who are the two it is asked to?

segments.text: Now look closely. The words are not singular. They are dual. Allah is speaking to two different creations at once. Can you guess which two? Comment your answer.
The word is rabbikumaa. Your Lord, spoken to two. And the surah names them outright, just a few verses later.
And there it is, stated plainly. O company of jinn and mankind. This surah was never speaking to you alone.
And for those who fear their Lord, Ar-Rahman promises not one paradise. Two gardens. And then, two more.
Everyone upon the earth will perish. And there will remain the Face of your Lord, full of majesty and honor.

data.heading: What it teaches
items: Its mercy reaches all creation, even the jinn.
Every blessing is a question: will you be grateful?
He promises four gardens to those who fear Him.
All things perish, but the Face of your Lord remains.

segments.text: Its mercy reaches every creation. Every blessing asks: will you be grateful, or deny it? Four gardens await those who fear Him. And all things fade, but His Face remains.
Mercy taught you to speak. Mercy gave you everything. So which of His favors will you deny? Read Surah Ar-Rahman.
segments.caption: Read Ar-Rahman.

segments.text: And our app, Ketabi, is a calm home for your faith. Get it free, at Ketabi Studio dot com.
segments.caption: Download Ketabi.

sources: Surah Ar-Rahman (55), 78 verses; the refrain 'So which of the favors of your Lord will you deny?' recurs 31 times
Quran 55:1-4 (the Most Merciful taught the Quran, created man, taught him speech) - auto-pulled

The refrain is in the DUAL (rabbikumaa / tukadhdhibaan), addressing two creations; named outright as jinn and mankind in 55:33 ('O company of jinn and mankind') - 55:33 auto-pulled; cf. 55:31 'al-thaqalan' (the two weighty ones)
Quran 55:46 ('two gardens') and 55:62 ('and besides these two, two more gardens') = four gardens for those who fear their Lord
Quran 55:26-27 (all on earth perish; the Face of your Lord remains) - auto-pulled
